name: Prefab Office Buildings
description:
Prefab office buildings sit in a useful middle ground: larger and more permanent than a movable office cabin, but simpler and quicker than a multi-floor office complex. SAMAN's prefab office building is a 30 ft × 12 ft × 9 ft steel-and-wood structure — about 360 sq ft of usable floor — that arrives factory-finished and ready to be divided into the office you actually need. It is priced at ₹4,15,000 (regular ₹4,25,000), inclusive of taxes, and is fabricated on our own lines, not bought in from a trader.

What is a prefab office building — and how is it different from a portable office cabin?

A prefab office building is an office structure built in sections at a factory and delivered ready to use, on a structural steel frame with insulated wall panels. Unlike a small movable office cabin, it is sized and finished as a building — a wider floor, fixed once placed, and designed to be split into several rooms rather than used as a single open box.

A 30×12 ft prefab office building serving as the project office on a highway construction site.

That distinction matters when you brief a supplier. A prefabricated office building is the right call when the workspace will stay put for years and needs to behave like a proper office: a reception, a couple of cabins, and a shared work area under one roof. A cabin suits a single user who relocates often; a building suits a team that settles in.

Inside the building: rooms, partitions and office fit-out

Inside the 30×12 ft office — partitions split it into reception, cabin and work area.

A 30 × 12 ft footprint is enough to lay out a working office rather than a single room. A common configuration is a reception or open desk area at the front, one or two enclosed cabins for managers, and a small meeting or records space at the rear — created with internal partitions rather than load-bearing walls, so the layout follows your team, not the structure. Tell us the split you want before fabrication and the partitions are positioned at the factory.

The shell is built for daily office use, not temporary shelter. Walls are insulated sandwich panels that hold back summer heat and keep the interior usable through the year; the steel frame is fabricated and welded at our plant; the roof is finished, the floor is laid, and windows are fitted for cross-ventilation. Inside, the unit is wired with a distribution board ready to connect to your supply, with provision for an AC point and a false ceiling. Because every prefab built office building leaves the factory finished, there is no panel assembly or wiring run happening on your premises — the unit is signed off before dispatch.

Prefab office building vs RCC: cost and time for a 360 sq ft office

The reason businesses choose prefab over conventional construction for an office this size is straightforward: it is faster, it carries a predictable cost per square foot, and it spares you the disruption of a brick-and-mortar site. A prefab metal office building of this footprint is ready in a fraction of the time an equivalent RCC office would take, with most of the work completed off-site while you carry on operating.

Prefab office building RCC / brick-and-mortar office
Build time ~2–4 weeks, mostly off-site 3–6 months on-site
Civil work Minimal — a level base/plinth Full foundation, masonry, curing
Cost basis Fixed quote (from ₹4,15,000) Variable; rises with delays
Relocation Can be lifted and moved Permanent — demolish to remove
Site disruption Low — delivered finished High — extended wet works

Set on a level base in days — no foundation, masonry or curing like an RCC office.

The ₹4,15,000 figure covers the 30 × 12 × 9 ft unit as specified above. Because it needs only a level base rather than a deep foundation, your total project cost stays close to the unit price — there is no separate civil contract quietly doubling the bill.

Choosing the right SAMAN office: this building, a movable cabin, or multi-floor modular

These products sit close together, so here is the quick way to choose. Pick this prefab office building when you want a fixed, multi-room office of around 360 sq ft that stays on one site. If you need a single, smaller office that you can lift and relocate as your work moves, a compact office cabin we finish in-house is the better-matched unit. If you prefer the container look for a branded or rugged setting, a container-style building of similar size covers that. And when your team has outgrown a single structure, a multi-floor office that expands module by module is the step up rather than a second standalone unit.

One standalone office building — the right pick before you need a multi-floor modular block.

Matching the product to the decision keeps the cost honest: you are not paying for relocation hardware you will never use, or for floors you do not yet need.

Delivery, installation and where this office sits in SAMAN's prefab range

Lead time runs to roughly two to four weeks depending on the partition layout and current factory load, after which the office is dispatched as a coordinated unit from our Bangalore or Greater Noida facility and set on your prepared base. Turnkey delivery and installation means the crew places, levels, and hands over the building ready to wire in — no on-site construction. A level plinth or compacted base is all the groundwork required.

Dispatched as one finished unit from Bangalore or Greater Noida and set on your base.

This office building is one variant in the wider prefab building line-up SAMAN manufactures — covering commercial, industrial and institutional structures from compact units up to large spans.

Frequently asked questions

What is the difference between a prefab office building and a portable office cabin?

Size, permanence and layout. A prefab office building is wider (here, 30 × 12 ft / 360 sq ft), stays on one site, and is built to be partitioned into rooms. A portable office cabin is a smaller single unit designed to be relocated. Choose the building for a settled team; the cabin for a single user who moves.

Does a prefab office building need a foundation or civil work?

Only a level base or plinth — not a deep RCC foundation. The steel-framed unit sits on a prepared, compacted surface, which is why installation is measured in days rather than months and why the project cost stays close to the unit price.

Can the office be partitioned into separate rooms?

Yes. The 30 × 12 ft floor is divided with internal partitions into reception, cabins and a meeting or work area. Specify the layout before fabrication and the partitions, electrical points and AC provision are fitted at the factory.

How much does it cost and how long does delivery take?

The 30 × 12 × 9 ft prefab office building is ₹4,15,000 (regular ₹4,25,000), inclusive of taxes. Typical lead time is two to four weeks, after which it is delivered and installed from our Bangalore or Greater Noida plant.

Get a project quote

Tell us your preferred internal layout, the city where you need the office, and your timeline — we'll send an itemised quote the same day.

Bangalore — Call +91 80886 85440 · WhatsApp +91 88616 22859
Delhi NCR — Call +91 87960 39938 · WhatsApp +91 97089 89937
short_description:
Prefab office building — 30×12×9 ft steel-built, partition-ready office
A factory-finished steel-and-wood office you divide into reception, cabins and a meeting room. Set on a level base in 2–4 weeks — no RCC foundation.
Size
30×12×9 ft · 360 sq ft
Capacity
6–10 staff, multi-room
Frame
Structural steel
Panel
Insulated sandwich
Price
₹4,15,000 incl. tax
Delivery
2–4 weeks
Floor Load
Office-grade
Relocations
Liftable, level base
Bangalore · Delhi NCR
Get a project quote
